coupling.comment: Blocking: The constants/index.ts hub adds coupling for 27 single-use constants that don't need extraction (see objection 1). Only 2 values are actually duplicated. ErrorBoundary/AppErrorBoundary introduce no new coupling — they wrap existing dependencies (Sentry, solid-js ErrorBoundary) already imported in app.tsx.

debuggability.comment: Good improvement: Per-route ErrorBoundary with Sentry capture means a crash on the home page won't destroy the session page, and the error will be reported. The plan doesn't add structured logging to the boundary, but Sentry integration provides reasonable traceability. Constants extraction has no debuggability impact.

convergence.comment: Blocking: Creating a parallel ErrorFallback instead of reusing the existing ErrorPage (371 line production component at pages/error.tsx) adds a divergent error display path that will need maintenance convergence later. Constants extraction is low-risk. Type declarations scope is undefined. Estimated 1-2 revision cycles needed for the three blocking objections.

surface_area.comment: Too wide: 11-12 files touched for changes that are mostly mechanical (string replacements) but still carry collision risk. The layout.tsx file alone is 2620 lines — touching it for a single URL replacement has a risk/reward imbalance. types/ipc.ts is undefined scope. Recommend splitting into 2 lanes: (a) ErrorBoundary + Window.api (3-4 files), (b) Constants extraction (4-6 files).

testability.comment: Moderate: ErrorBoundary can be tested with a component that throws within a test renderer. Constants are trivially testable (import and assert). URL replacements need visual/functional verification by running the app. ErrorFallback would need its own tests if not reusing ErrorPage (which already has testable coverage via the ensureFatalErrorRecorded path).

error_clarity.comment: Modest improvement: Per-route ErrorBoundary improves error isolation — instead of a blank white screen, the user sees a friendly fallback for the crashed route while other routes remain functional. The plan doesn't add diagnostic annotations (span tags, structured logs) to the error path. Sentry integration provides off-site diagnosis but weak local debuggability.

reversibility.comment: Mixed: ErrorBoundary wrapping per-route can be reverted individually per <Route>. Constants extraction is one-way — once 8 files import from constants/index.ts, reverting requires touching all 8. types/ipc.ts would similarly create dependencies. The DESKTOP_FEEDBACK_URL changes in 3 files are mechanical reverts. Recommend keeping each work item in separate commits for clean revert isolation.

objections.file_evidence: Plan proposes constants/index.ts with BASE_URL, DOCS_URL, FAVICON_URL, FAVICON_SVG_URL, ZEN_URL (all single-use). STORAGE_KEYS with LANGUAGE, THEME_ID, THEME_CSS_LIGHT, THEME_CSS_DARK (all single-use in their owning modules). TIMEOUTS extracted from server-health.ts where they're already cleanly scoped as local constants.
objections.summary: Premature extraction of 27 single-use constants to a shared module creates a hub dependency pattern without eliminating any duplication. AGENTS.md: 'Do not extract single-use helpers preemptively.'
objections.repair: Scope constants/index.ts to only actually-duplicated values: DESKTOP_FEEDBACK_URL (3 sites), DEFAULT_URL (2 sites). Leave TIMEOUTS, STORAGE_KEYS, and single-use URL constants local to their owning modules.

objections.file_evidence: Plan section 'Files to Create #4: packages/app/src/types/ipc.ts' — described as 'Shared IPC types module' with no type definitions, no imports, no usage sites. Existing type boundaries are at app.tsx:70-87 (Window.api), types/agent.ts, types/mcp.ts.
objections.summary: types/ipc.ts has no concrete spec — what types does it contain? How does it relate to existing type modules? Creating it without defining its contents is undefined scope that may result in unused code or require a second revision to fix.
objections.repair: Defer types/ipc.ts. Add a concrete type specification to the plan, or extend existing modules (app.tsx Window.api, types/agent.ts) instead.

objections.file_evidence: Plan section 'Files to Create #3: packages/app/src/components/error-fallback.tsx' — 'A themed error fallback that shows error message, retry button, and report button'. Existing ErrorPage at packages/app/src/pages/error.tsx:221 (371 lines) already implements error display, formatting, Sentry recording, update checking, and debug log export.
objections.summary: Creating a new ErrorFallback duplicates the existing ErrorPage component's functionality, creating two maintenance paths that will diverge. The ErrorPage already accepts `{ error: unknown }`, matching the ErrorBoundary fallback signature exactly.
objections.repair: Remove ErrorFallback from the plan. Use the existing ErrorPage component as the fallback for per-route ErrorBoundary instances. Optionally wrap it with additional context (retry handler, close button) that are missing from ErrorPage's current interface.

conditions: Condition A: Scope down constants/index.ts to only actually-duplicated constants (DESKTOP_FEEDBACK_URL, DEFAULT_URL). All single-use values stay in their owning modules.
Condition B: Defer types/ipc.ts creation until a concrete type specification is added to the plan.
Condition C: Remove ErrorFallback from the plan. Use the existing ErrorPage component as the ErrorBoundary fallback.
Condition D: Split the work into at least 2 lanes — (1) ErrorBoundary + Window.api type fixes, (2) Constants extraction — so they can be independently published and reverted.

follow_up_issues.summary: Catalog the remaining unextracted hardcoded opencode.ai URLs across the codebase (favicon.svg in helpers.ts, /docs in desktop-menu.ts, /docs/providers/ in dialog-custom-provider.tsx, /docs/themes/ in settings-general.tsx, /zen in dialog-connect-provider.tsx) and either extract them to constants/index.ts in a follow-up or document them as intentional single-use values.

Evaluate whether ErrorPage needs a retry handler prop for ErrorBoundary usage. Currently ErrorPage accepts only `{ error: unknown }`; a retry callback would let it serve as a proper ErrorBoundary fallback with recovery.

claim_atom_verification.notes: Claim atom 1 (constants centralization) is blocked by Condition A. Claim atoms 2-3 (ErrorBoundary, ErrorFallback) are partially blocked by Condition C. The remaining 5 claim atoms were not fully enumerated in the plan artifact (8 claimed but only 3 shown in the artifact).
